name: Compound Interest Calculator
metaTitle: Compound Interest Calculator — Real Returns After Tax & Inflation | Financial Simulators
metaDescription: See how compound interest really grows your money after tax and inflation. Works for lump sum, monthly SIP, or both. Client-side, no PII.
shortDescription: Real compound growth after tax & inflation

faq.question: How does compound interest work?
faq.answer: Compound interest means you earn interest on interest. ₹1L at 12% compounded annually becomes ₹3.11L in 10 years — not ₹2.2L (which is simple interest). The difference grows exponentially with time: at 20 years, compound gives ₹9.65L vs ₹3.4L simple.

faq.question: What is the real return on compound interest?
faq.answer: A 12% nominal return with 6% inflation and 30% tax on gains gives approximately 5.7% real return. But if you invest via equity mutual funds (LTCG tax at 12.5% above ₹1.25L), your real return is closer to 9.5%.

faq.question: Monthly vs annual compounding — how much difference?
faq.answer: On ₹10L at 12% for 10 years: annual compounding gives ₹31.06L, monthly gives ₹33.01L — about ₹2L more. The difference grows with tenure: at 20 years, monthly compounding gives ~₹19L more.

faq.question: Should I include tax in my compound interest calculation?
faq.answer: Yes. A 12% return with 30% tax on gains gives only ~9.7% effective return. Over 20 years, this difference means ₹30-40L less on a ₹10L investment. Always calculate post-tax returns.
